monster_usage_guide.title: How to Use a Monster
monster_usage_guide.overview: Every monster is a font of adventure. Monsters represent the weird, the whimsical, the majestic, and the macabre. A monster has a stat block that contains the rules necessary to use it in the game.
monster_usage_guide.description: Stat blocks are divided into distinct parts corresponding to a standard monster stat block structure. Each part provides specific information needed to run the monster in combat and roleplay scenarios.

name_and_general_details.title: Name and General Details
name_and_general_details.description: The name of the monster is followed by its size, creature type (along with any descriptive tags), and alignment.
components: Size Category (Tiny, Small, Medium, Large, Huge, Gargantuan)
name_and_general_details.example: Vampire Familiar - Medium or Small Humanoid, Neutral Evil

combat_highlights.title: Combat Highlights
combat_highlights.description: Armor Class, Hit Points, Speed, and Initiative are provided here for quick reference during combat.

ability_scores.title: Ability Scores
ability_scores.description: A monster's ability scores, ability modifiers, and saving throws are listed here.

defenses.title: Defenses and Attributes
defenses.description: Damage resistances, vulnerabilities, immunities, condition immunities, and senses are listed here.

actions_and_reactions.title: Actions and Reactions
actions_and_reactions.description: All the actions, bonus actions, and reactions the monster can take are described here.

legendary_actions.title: Legendary Actions
legendary_actions.description: Powerful monsters have legendary actions that allow them to act outside their turn. These are detailed separately and typically require resource spending.
rules: Legendary creatures typically get 3 legendary action options per turn
Can move up to half speed without using an action
Can take one legendary action at a time, only at the end of another creature's turn
Dies at end of next turn if it can't take actions on its own turn

lair_actions.title: Lair Actions
lair_actions.description: Lair actions are special actions that a monster can take only while in its lair, typically on an initiative count of 20, losing ties.
rules: Can only be taken while in the creature's lair
Can't take lair actions while incapacitated or dead

creature_types.title: Creature Types
creature_types.description: Each monster has a tag that identifies the type of creature it is. Certain spells, magic items, class features, and other effects in the game interact in special ways with creatures of a particular type.

types.description: Utterly alien beings with bizarre anatomy and strange powers
Non-Humanoid natural creatures and most giant animals
Magical creatures with ties to the Upper Planes
Creatures tied to the Feywild or the forces of nature
Creatures tied to terrifying Lower Planes
People defined by their roles and professions

hit_dice_by_size.title: Hit Dice by Size
hit_dice_by_size.description: A monster's size typically determines the die used to calculate its Hit Points

initiative.title: Initiative
initiative.description: The Initiative entry specifies the monster's Initiative modifier followed by the monster's Initiative score in parentheses.
usage: Use the modifier when you roll to determine a monster's Initiative
Use the score in parentheses as the monster's Initiative in combat if you don't want to roll
initiative.calculation: A monster's Initiative modifier is typically equal to its Dexterity modifier, but some monsters have additional modifiers, such as Proficiency Bonus, applied to that number

armor_class.title: Armor Class
armor_class.description: A monster's Armor Class (AC) includes its natural armor, Dexterity, gear, and other defenses.

ability_scores.title: Ability Scores
ability_scores.description: Every monster has six ability scores along with corresponding ability score modifiers and saving throw modifiers.

skills.title: Skills
skills.description: The Skills entry specifies a monster's skill proficiencies, if any.
skills.calculation: A skill bonus is the sum of a monster's relevant ability modifier and its Proficiency Bonus. Other modifiers might apply.

resistances.title: Damage Resistances
resistances.description: The monster takes half damage from the listed damage type

vulnerabilities.title: Damage Vulnerabilities
vulnerabilities.description: The monster takes double damage from the listed damage type

damage_immunities.title: Damage Immunities
damage_immunities.description: The monster takes no damage from the listed damage type

condition_immunities.title: Condition Immunities
condition_immunities.description: The monster cannot be affected by the listed condition

gear.title: Gear
gear.description: Monsters have proficiency with their equipment. If a monster has equipment that can be given away or retrieved, the items are listed in the Gear entry.
guidelines: The monster's stat block might include special flourishes that happen when the monster uses an item
When used by someone else, a retrievable item uses its standard Equipment rules
The Gear entry doesn't necessarily list all of a monster's equipment (e.g., clothes are assumed but not listed)
Equipment mentioned outside the Gear entry is considered supernatural or highly specialized and is unusable after defeat
gear.ammunition: A monster that requires ammunition to make ranged attacks carries the necessary ammunition

equipping_monsters.title: Equipping a Monster with Other Items
guidance: You decide how much of a monster's equipment is recoverable after the creature is slain
You decide whether that equipment is still usable after defeat
Beware of giving a monster combat-oriented magic items, since those might alter the monster's Challenge Rating
If you do give a monster a magic item, the monster can have Attunement with magic items as noted in Equipment
A monster with a class tag after its creature type is considered a member of that class for Attunement purposes

senses.title: Senses
senses.description: Monsters have special senses that allow them to perceive their environment

common_senses.description: See in darkness out to a specified range (typically 30-120 ft)
Perceive surroundings without relying on sight
See in darkness and magical darkness, detect invisible creatures and objects
Detect vibrations through a solid surface or substance
A creature's perception when not actively looking (10 + Wisdom modifier + proficiency bonus if applicable)

languages.title: Languages
languages.description: Lists the languages a monster can speak and understand
languages.note: Some monsters understand languages but can't speak them. Some monsters can't communicate verbally at all.
languages.telepathy: Some monsters have telepathic ability allowing thought-based communication

challenge_rating.title: Challenge Rating
challenge_rating.description: The Challenge Rating (CR) indicates how difficult the monster is to defeat
components: Experience Points (XP) awarded for defeating the monster
Proficiency Bonus (PB) derived from the CR
challenge_rating.usage: Used for encounter balancing and determining appropriate monster difficulty for a party

swarm_traits.title: Swarm Traits
swarm_traits.description: Swarms have special mechanics distinguishing them from single creatures
key_features: Can occupy another creature's space and vice versa
Can move through openings large enough for component creatures
Cannot regain hit points or gain temporary hit points
Takes reduced damage when bloodied (below half HP)
Has damage resistances to physical damage types

pack_tactics.title: Pack Tactics
pack_tactics.description: A creature has advantage on attack rolls against a creature if at least one of the creature's allies is within 5 feet of that creature and the ally doesn't have the Incapacitated condition
pack_tactics.usage: Common for predators and group hunters

legendary_actions_mechanics.title: Legendary Actions
legendary_actions_mechanics.description: Powerful monsters can act outside their turn
rules: A monster can take 3 legendary actions per turn outside its turn
A monster can move up to half its speed without using an action
Can take 1 legendary action at a time at the end of another creature's turn
A monster can't take legendary actions if it's incapacitated or dies

bonus_actions.title: Bonus Actions
bonus_actions.description: If a monster has Bonus Action options, they are listed in this section. Bonus Actions can be taken in addition to the monster's action and movement on each turn.
characteristics: Bonus actions are separate from standard actions
A creature can use one bonus action per turn unless stated otherwise
Can be used for swift attacks, special abilities, or mobility
Must be available and usable without spending the action

common_bonus_actions.description: Many monsters can make an additional weapon attack as a bonus action
Move up to the monster's speed as a bonus action
Withdraw from combat without provoking opportunity attacks
Cast a spell with a casting time of 1 bonus action
Use a special ability unique to the monster

reactions.title: Reactions
reactions.description: If the monster has Reaction options, those are listed in this section along with their triggers.
mechanics: Reactions can be taken in response to a specific trigger
A creature typically gets one reaction per round
Resets at the start of the creature's turn
reactions.trigger_specification: Each reaction specifies the condition or action that triggers it

common_reactions.description: Make a melee weapon attack against a creature leaving reach

common_reactions.trigger: Creature is hit by an attack
common_reactions.description: Add a bonus to AC against the attack, potentially negating the hit
Gain advantage on the saving throw or potentially negate the spell entirely

common_reactions.trigger: Targeted by an effect requiring a Dexterity save
common_reactions.description: Take no damage on success, half damage on failure
Make an immediate attack in response to a missed attack

legendary_actions_detailed.title: Legendary Actions - Detailed
legendary_actions_detailed.description: If the monster has Legendary Action options, those are listed in this section. A Legendary Action is an action that a monster can take immediately after another creature's turn.
rules: Only one legendary action can be taken at a time
Only taken after another creature's turn ends
Monster can't take legendary action if it has Incapacitated condition or can't take actions
Monster has a limited number of legendary action uses
usage: Monster expends one use whenever it takes a legendary action
Monster regains all expended uses at the start of each of its turns
legendary_actions_detailed.movement: Legendary creature can move up to half its speed without using an action

legendary_resistance.title: Legendary Resistance
legendary_resistance.description: Many powerful creatures have legendary resistance, which lets them reroll a saving throw they fail.
mechanics: When the creature fails a saving throw, it can choose to succeed instead
It can use this ability a number of times equal to its Charisma modifier (minimum 1)
The creature regains expended uses of legendary resistance when it finishes a long rest

common_legendary_actions.description: Make a weapon attack or cast a cantrip
Move up to half the creature's speed without provoking opportunity attacks
Cast a spell that has a casting time of 1 action or reaction
Gain resistance to damage, dodge attacks, or trigger protective ability
Trigger a special ability unique to the creature

limited_usage.title: Limited Usage
limited_usage.description: Some parts of a stat block have restrictions on the number of times they can be used. Here are the most common ways that usage is limited:

restrictions.description: The stat block part can be used a certain number of times (represented by X) and the monster must finish a Long Rest to regain expended uses.
restrictions.example: A Reaction that includes '1/Day' means the Reaction can be taken once and the monster must finish a Long Rest to take it again.
mechanics: Uses reset after Long Rest only

restrictions.description: A monster can use the stat block part once. At the start of each of the monster's turns, roll 1d6. If the roll is within the number range given in the notation (represented by X–Y), the monster regains the use of that part.
restrictions.recharge_mechanics: Recharges when the monster finishes a Short or Long Rest as well
restrictions.example: 'Recharge 5–6' in an action means a monster can take the action once. Then, at the start of each of the monster's turns, it regains the use of that action if it rolls a 5 or 6 on 1d6.

common_recharge_ranges.typical_use: Less powerful but still limited abilities

restrictions.type: Recharge after a Short or Long Rest
restrictions.notation: Recharge after a Short or Long Rest
restrictions.description: The monster can use the stat block part once and must then finish a Short or Long Rest to use it again.
mechanics: Uses reset after either a Short Rest (1 hour) or Long Rest (8 hours)
Commonly used for powerful but not game-breaking abilities

experience_points.title: Experience Points
experience_points.description: The number of Experience Points (XP) a monster is worth is based on its CR. XP is awarded for defeating the monster in combat or otherwise neutralizing it.
experience_points.summoned_creatures: Unless a rule says otherwise, a monster summoned by a spell or another magical ability is worth the XP noted in its stat block.

proficiency_bonus.title: Proficiency Bonus
proficiency_bonus.description: A monster's Proficiency Bonus (PB) is determined by its CR and is reflected in saving throws, skills, and other statistics where the monster's exceptional aptitude is a factor.
proficiency_bonus.application: Proficiency bonus applies to attack rolls, saving throws, skill checks, and ability checks where the monster has proficiency

running_monsters.title: Running a Monster
running_monsters.overview: To ensure a monster acts in accordance with its Challenge Rating, follow these rules during combat:

guidelines.description: If the monster has a special ability that deals a lot of damage but has a limited number of uses, such as a recharging breath weapon or a spell it can cast only once per day, have it use that special ability as quickly and as often as possible.
If the monster has Multiattack, have it use Multiattack on any of its turns in which it's not using one of its more powerful abilities.
If the monster has Bonus Actions, Reactions, or Legendary Actions in its stat block, make sure it uses them as often as it can.

monster_actions.title: Monster Actions
monster_actions.overview: A monster can take the actions described in its stat block or take one of the actions available to all creatures, as described in the general Playing the Game rules.

attack_notation.title: Attack Notation
attack_notation.description: The entry for a monster's attack identifies whether the attack is a melee or a ranged attack and then provides the attack roll's bonus, its reach or range, and what happens on a hit.
attack_notation.note: An attack is against one target unless its entry says otherwise

hit_notation.title: Hit Notation
hit_notation.description: Any damage dealt or other effects that occur as a result of an attack hitting a target are described after the 'Hit:' notation.

miss_notation.title: Miss Notation
miss_notation.description: If an attack has an effect that occurs on a miss, that information follows the 'Miss:' notation.

hit_or_miss_notation.title: Hit or Miss Notation
hit_or_miss_notation.description: If an attack has an effect that occurs regardless of whether it hits or misses its target, that information follows the 'Hit or Miss:' notation.

saving_throw_effects.title: Saving Throw Effect Notation
saving_throw_effects.description: If an effect forces a saving throw, the effect identifies the kind of save required and then provides the save's DC, a description of which creatures make the save, and an explanation of what happens on a failed or successful save.
saving_throw_effects.half_damage_rule: 'Half damage only' on a successful save means the target takes half as much damage (round down) as targets that fail the save, while also ignoring all other parts of the effect.

damage_notation.title: Damage Notation
damage_notation.description: A stat block usually provides both a number and a die expression for each instance of damage.
damage_notation.format: For example, an attack might deal 4 (1d4 + 2) damage on a hit
damage_notation.usage: You decide whether to use the number or the die expression in parentheses; don't use both

multiattack_mechanics.title: Multiattack
multiattack_mechanics.description: Some creatures can make more than one attack when they take the Attack action. Such creatures have the Multiattack entry in the 'Actions' section of their stat block.
multiattack_mechanics.details: This entry details the attacks a creature can make, as well as any additional abilities it can use, as part of the Attack action.
examples: Makes one bite and two claw attacks
Makes one attack with each weapon it wields

spellcasting.title: Spellcasting
spellcasting.description: If a monster can cast any spells, its stat block lists the spells and provides the monster's spellcasting ability, spell save DC (if any spells require a saving throw), and spell attack bonus (if any spells require an attack roll).
spellcasting.spell_levels: Unless noted otherwise, a spell of level 1 or higher is always cast at its lowest possible level and can't be cast at a higher level.
spellcasting.special_restrictions: A monster's spell can have special rules or restrictions. For example, a green hag can cast the Invisibility spell, but the spell has a 'self only' restriction, which means the spell affects only the hag.

components.title: Spell Components
components.description: The Spellcasting trait notes whether the monster's spellcasting ignores the need for certain spell components.
guidelines: If any spell components are required, describe the monster's use of Verbal, Somatic, or Material components to signal to characters that it is casting a spell
A monster that requires Material components has them

casting_times.title: Casting Times of 1+ Minutes
casting_times.description: If a spell has a casting time of 1 minute or more yet is listed in a spellcasting action, the monster doesn't cast the spell in just one action unless the action's description states otherwise.
rules: The monster must take the Magic action on each of its turns
The monster must maintain Concentration to cast the spell
This follows the rules described in the Rules Glossary

monster_traits.title: Monster Traits
monster_traits.description: A monster's traits, if any, are features that are active at all times or in certain situations.

types.description: Passive features that are constantly in effect (e.g., Darkvision, Damage Resistances, Immunities)
Features that activate under specific circumstances (e.g., Pack Tactics, Swarm mechanics)
Features that activate when a specific trigger occurs (e.g., reactions, legendary action triggers)

telepathy_rules.title: Telepathy
telepathy_rules.description: Telepathy is a magical ability that allows a creature to communicate mentally with another creature within a specified range.
mechanics: Mental communication doesn't require shared language
Creatures must be able to understand each other's thoughts (some creatures have restrictions)
Range is specified in the creature's telepathy ability
Telepathy usually works on the same plane of existence only
